It was around 1:00 a.m. when I responded to a domestic call and what struck me immediately wasn't the chaos of the domestic, it was the silence because the woman who met us at the door was calm, but too calm. Like this wasn't her first time giving officers an after midnight tour of her toxic relationship. Now she didn't have any physical injuries, but after my 15 minutes of being there and temporarily solving her and her boyfriend's issue, I honestly was ready to go because these situations whether alcohol is involved or not are very personal because it's the only kind of call where everyone hates you by the end of it. The victim's ashamed and the suspect's furious and both of them just want you to go. And every officer has that one call they can't forget because if you've been doing this long enough, these calls start to stack up. You got the angry drunk, the woman with the split lip who swore it was an accident or the husband who was almost stabbed by his wife who's begging you not to arrest her. It's honestly repetition and the repetition wears you down. And even though you and I just watched this bar disturbance, this isn't a TV show. You're watching this from the safety of a screen, maybe your phone, maybe you're on a couch. But the officers in this video, they're not watching, they're living it. And this isn't about adrenaline, it's honestly about survival and it starts like a bad joke because you walk into this bar, you don't know if the loudest guy in the room has a knife in his waistband or a gun in the glove box. You don't know if his girlfriend's going to help him calm down or jump on your back the moment you decide to go hands on. It's not just dangerous, it's volatile. And it only takes a moment for it to turn deadly. Now, in my opinion, these officers did a great job, but ask yourself this, how many jobs demand that you stay composed while someone tries to beat you unconscious and how many careers expect perfection in chaos and punish you for anything less? The male was charged with criminal mischief causing damage to property costing more than $1,000 for damaging the inside of the squad car when he was kicking, resisting, obstructing without violence, and disorderly intoxication, totaling a bond of $2,763. His girlfriend was charged with resisting without violence. They are both innocent unless proven guilty in a court of law.
